sections.title: Informed Consent Summary
sections.text:
# Informed Consent Summary

Participants will be informed that the study includes screening, baseline, Week 4, Week 8, Week 12 and follow-up visits. The main symptom score assessment is expected at Week 12.

Participants will also be informed about potential risks, safety laboratory tests and their right to withdraw from the study at any time.

sections.title: Objectives and Endpoints
sections.text:
# Objectives and Endpoints

## 2.1 Primary Objective

To assess the effect of TC-001 compared with placebo on symptom improvement.

## 2.2 Primary Endpoint

The primary endpoint is the change from baseline in Symptom Severity Score at Week 12.

## 2.3 Secondary Endpoints

Secondary endpoints include change from baseline in quality-of-life score at Week 12 and responder rate at Week 12.

sections.title: Protocol Synopsis
sections.text:
# Protocol Synopsis

This synthetic Phase II study evaluates investigational product TC-001 in adults aged 18 to 65 years. Approximately 120 participants will be randomized in a 1:1 ratio to TC-001 or placebo.

The primary objective is to evaluate whether TC-001 improves the Symptom Severity Score compared with placebo. The primary endpoint is the change from baseline in Symptom Severity Score at Week 12.

Participants will attend screening, baseline, Week 4, Week 8, Week 12 and follow-up visits. The Week 12 visit is the main efficacy assessment visit for the primary endpoint.

Safety assessments include adverse events, laboratory tests and vital signs throughout the study.

sections.title: SAP Excerpt
sections.text:
# SAP Excerpt

The primary analysis will use the full analysis set. The primary endpoint is the change from baseline in Symptom Severity Score at Week 12.

Sensitivity analyses may include alternative handling of missing data. The analysis model will include treatment group and baseline score.

sections.text:
# Schedule of Activities

| Activity | Screening | Baseline | Week 4 | Week 8 | Week 12 | Week 16 | Follow-up |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Informed consent | X |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Eligibility review | X | X |  |  |  |  |  |
| Study treatment |  | X | X | X | X |  |  |
| Symptom Severity Score |  | X | X | X |  | X |  |
| Safety laboratory tests | X | X | X | X | X | X | X |

Note: This table intentionally contains an injected defect. The primary endpoint assessment is shown at Week 16 although the confirmed fact sheet currently states Week 12.

sections.title: Statistical Principles
sections.text:
# Statistical Principles

The primary efficacy analysis will compare the change from baseline in Symptom Severity Score at Week 12 between treatment groups.

The full analysis set includes all randomized participants who receive at least one dose of study treatment. The safety set includes all participants who receive at least one dose of study treatment and have at least one post-baseline safety assessment.

The primary endpoint will be analyzed using an analysis of covariance model with treatment group and baseline score as covariates.
